prompts:
/plan "Plan: Fix GeoRanker Webhook Result Storage

Error 1: Missing grid_x and grid_y
The webhook creates results without grid_x and grid_y

Additional information required to send back with webhooks
- top3_count - (count of geopoints that contain our google place_id with a rank of 1 to 3)
- top10_count - (count of geopoints that do contain our google place_id with a rank of 1 to 10)
- not_ranked_count (count of geopoints that do not contain our google place_id)

Some meta data we are sending back:
- not_found_count (duplicate of not_ranked_count)
- best_rank (best rank for our google place_id across all geopoints)
- worst_rank (worst rank for our google place_id across all geopoints, return 0 if not found)

When we create the geopoints from the geosearch give each one an gridX and gridY value based on x and y coordinates where the bottom left coordinate is (0,0) and the top right coordinate is (N,N) the largest value, start from the bottom row incrementing from left to right. Each geopoint search should know the (x,y) coordinate it has for that particular search, that grid coordinate changes over time and is only relevant in the context of the current search.

the backend code will need to update the function that splits the geosearch into geogrids to implement this feature."
can we add the average rank for the google place_id to the calculated value, then update the documentation including the swagger documentation
